title: Contend for faith; judgment on ungodly
summary_text: Jude servant Jesus brother James—to called sanctified kept—mercy peace love multiplied—contend earnestly faith delivered—certain men crept turning grace licentiousness denying Master—examples Israel angels Sodom dreamers—Michael disputed devil—woe way Cain error Balaam Korah—spots love feasts—Enoch prophecy—keep yourselves love God mercy life.
key_points: Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ, and brother of James.
I thought it necessary to write to you exhorting you to contend earnestly for the faith which was once for all delivered to the saints.
For there are certain men who crept in secretly.
I desire to remind you, though you already know this.
To these people is reserved the blackness of darkness forever.
Now to him who is able to keep them from stumbling.
evidence_phrases: certain men who crept in secretly
Now to him who is able to keep them from stumbling
